data.snippet: Pro-democracy media tycoon Jimmy Lai's fraud conviction and prison sentence were overturned by a Hong Kong court on Thursday, in a surprise legal decision that comes soon after Lai was jailed for 20 years on a separate national security charge.

image.text: Media mogul Jimmy Lai, founder of Apple Daily, arrives the Court of Final Appeal by prison van in Hong Kong, China February 9, 2021.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: A Hong Kong court sentenced the father of a wanted pro-democracy activist to eight months in prison on Thursday under the city’s national security law, after he attempted to terminate her insurance policy and withdraw the funds.
relatedHeadlines.text: Father of Hong Kong activist sentenced to eight months in jail under national security law

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Hong Kong's Court of Appeal on Thursday overturned a lower court ruling against jailed pro-democracy media tycoon Jimmy Lai's conviction and sentence for a fraud offence, saying the judge in the case had "erred." The ruling came just weeks after Lai was sentenced to 20 years in jail on separate charges on two counts of conspiracy to collude with foreign forces and one for publishing seditious materials.
relatedHeadlines.text: TIMELINE-Hong Kong's legal clampdown on Jimmy Lai, tycoon and China critic

data.text: Hong Kong court overturns China critic Jimmy Lai's fraud conviction in rare legal victory

data.snippet: A large Chinese military drone has conducted regular flights over the South China Sea in recent months while transmitting false transponder signals that made it appear to be other aircraft, including a sanctioned Belarusian cargo plane and a British Typhoon fighter jet.

image.text: A satellite image shows large drones at Qionghai Boao International Airport on Hainan Island, China, July 18, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: China is the real threat to security and is hypocritically claiming to uphold U.N. principles of peace, Taiwan Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung said on Sunday in a rebuff to comments by China's top diplomat at the Munich Security Conference.
relatedHeadlines.text: China is the real threat, Taiwan says in rebuff to Munich speech

data.text: INSIGHT-How China is masking drone flights in potential Taiwan rehearsal

data.snippet: Syngenta Group plans to launch an up to $10 billion stock market listing in Hong Kong in the second quarter of this year and has hired banks to manage the initial public offering, two people with knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

image.text: A logo is seen at the headquarters of agricultural chemical maker Syngenta in Basel, Switzerland January 30, 2020.

data.text: Syngenta plans to launch up to $10 billion Hong Kong IPO in second quarter, sources say

data.snippet: China's largest search engine operator Baidu reported a 4% revenue drop for its December quarter on Thursday, as persistent weakness in its mainstay advertising business offset strong growth in its cloud business.

image.text: A Baidu logo appears in this illustration taken August 18, 2025.

data.text: China's Baidu revenue falls as AI cloud growth fails to offset ad weakness

data.snippet: With Washington raising barriers, China's push to shift trade toward Europe is running into one big demand: let the yuan rise against the euro and level the playing field.

image.text: Euro and Yuan banknotes are seen in this picture illustration taken May 8, 2017.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: China's yuan firmed to a fresh 34-month high against the U.S. dollar on Thursday despite the central bank signalling it wanted to curb the currency's rapid gains, as seasonal settlement demand underpinned a sustained rally.
relatedHeadlines.text: Yuan strengthens past 6.85 level against dollar despite PBOC's caution

data.text: ROI-To win Europe's embrace, China needs to uncap the yuan: Mike Dolan

data.snippet: The United States has warned Syria against relying on Chinese technology in its telecommunications sector, arguing it conflicts with U.S. interests and threatens U.S. national security, according to three sources familiar with the matter.

image.text: Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa attends the Ministry of Awqaf conference titled "Unity of Islamic Discourse" at the Conference Palace in Damascus, Syria, February 16, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: DeepSeek, the Chinese artificial intelligence lab whose low-cost model rattled global markets last year, has not shown U.S. chipmakers its upcoming flagship model for performance optimization, two sources familiar with the matter said, breaking from standard industry practice ahead of a major model update.
relatedHeadlines.text: EXCLUSIVE-DeepSeek withholds latest AI model from US chipmakers including Nvidia, sources say

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Google disrupted a Chinese-linked hacking group that breached at least 53 organizations across 42 countries, the company said Wednesday.
relatedHeadlines.text: Google disrupts Chinese-linked hackers that attacked 53 groups globally

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Washington presses Syria to shift from Chinese telecom systems

data.snippet: Chinese lawmakers have stripped five People's Liberation Army generals of their membership of parliament, a statement from the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress showed on Thursday.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Chinese authorities removed Liu Shaoyun from the post of the head of the military court, state radio reported on Thursday, without explaining the reason for the removal or naming a successor.
relatedHeadlines.text: China removes military court head Liu Shaoyun, state media reports

relatedHeadlines.snippet:
China on Thursday removed
Wang Xiangxi from the post of emergency management minister,
state media reported, after the country's anti-graft watchdog
announced a probe into Wang in January citing suspected "serious
violations of discipline and law."

 (Reporting by Beijing Newsroom)
 ((Xiuhao.Chen@thomsonreuters.com;))

nP8N3YS07J
relatedHeadlines.text: China removes emergency management minister, state media reports

relatedHeadlines.snippet: China's ongoing military corruption purges are leaving serious deficiencies in its command structure and are likely to have hampered the readiness of its rapidly modernising armed forces, a leading defence research centre said on Tuesday.
relatedHeadlines.text: China military purge taking toll on command and readiness, study finds

data.text: China removes five generals from national lawmaking body

data.snippet: In the days surrounding Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's February election win, several dozen X accounts linked to a Chinese misinformation campaign attacked her deeply conservative views and hawkish approach to China, said a U.S. research institute focused on national security and foreign policy.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: The U.S. has arrested and charged a former U.S. Air Force fighter jet pilot with allegedly training China's military, highlighting Beijing's persistent efforts to modernize its armed forces by siphoning off U.S. secrets.
relatedHeadlines.text: US charges former F-35 pilot for training China's air force

data.text: Chinese influence operation targets Japan elections, Trump, other countries, US foundation says

data.snippet: Hong Kong's stock exchange said on Thursday that profit for 2025 surged to a record on buoyant trading activity amid a rush of firms going public and as investors lifted their exposure to Chinese assets - trends that are expected to continue.

image.text: The logo of Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing (HKEX) is pictured in its office, in Hong Kong, China, June 10, 2025.

data.text: HKEX posts record annual profit, says investors continue to diversify into China assets

data.snippet: China's central bank published rules on Thursday to regulate and support cross-border yuan financing businesses, as part of efforts to promote the global use of the Chinese currency.

image.text: Chinese 100 yuan banknotes are seen in this picture illustration created in Shanghai on January 17 , 2011.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: China's yuan firmed to a near three-year high against the U.S. dollar on Thursday despite the central bank's signal aimed at curbing the currency's rapid gains, as seasonal settlement demand continued to underpin a sustained rally.
relatedHeadlines.text: Yuan nears strongest level in three years against dollar despite PBOC's caution

data.text: China central bank publishes rules to support cross-border yuan lending

data.snippet: Jiuyuan Genetic Biopharmaceutical has applied for approval to sell its experimental biosimilar version of Novo Nordisk's blockbuster weight-loss drug Wegovy in China, the Chinese drugmaker said Wednesday.
data.text: China's Jiuyuan Genetic seeks approval for Wegovy biosimilar

data.snippet: China stocks closed lower on Thursday, snapping a two-session post-Lunar New Year rally, as investors held off on major moves ahead of an annual parliamentary meeting next week.

image.text: A woman cycles past the Beijing Stock Exchange building adorned with Lunar New Year decorations, on the Financial Street in Beijing, China February 8, 2024.

data.text: China stocks end lower as traders hold off before key policy meeting

data.snippet: Hong Kong billionaire Li Ka-shing's empire is selling UK Power to French utility Engie for $14 bln. It frees up capital for more deals. With CK flagships trading at just 40% of book value, however, finding a better investment than returning it to shareholders will be tough.

image.text: Hong Kong tycoon Li Ka-shing listens to his son Victor Li during a news conference in Hong Kong, China, March 16, 2018.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: French utility Engie will buy electricity distributor UK Power Networks for 10.5 billion pounds ($14.2 billion) in a bid to expand its power business, the company said on Wednesday as it reported flat full-year earnings and revised upward its outlook for the coming years.
relatedHeadlines.text: France's Engie to buy UK power grid from Hong Kong conglomerate for $14 billion

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Li clan amps up quest for a simple sprawl discount

data.snippet: One of the least talked about dynamics of the global crude oil market is the role China plays in setting a floor and a ceiling for prices.

image.text: A man is seen at an exit of the refinery plants of Chambroad Petrochemicals in Binzhou, Shandong province, China October 24, 2019.

data.text: ROI-China is shifting crude oil buying in response to price rally: Russell

data.snippet: China's lithium price surged on Thursday after Zimbabwe suspended raw mineral exports, sparking concerns over supply disruptions, at a time when growing energy storage demand is projected to drive a boom in the market.

image.text: A block with the symbol, atomic number and mass number of Lithium (Li) element, in this illustration taken January 21, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: China's dominance of critical mineral supply chains is not as absolute as it may appear. China accounted for 78% of global refined output of the battery metal in 2024, according to the International Energy Agency.
relatedHeadlines.text: ROI-Congo's cobalt curbs expose China's critical metals weak spot: Andy Home

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Some Chinese steelmakers in the northern region will need to trim production by at least 30% from March 4 to ensure clean air quality during the annual parliamentary meeting, analysts and consultancies said.
relatedHeadlines.text: Some Chinese steelmakers asked to cut production as annual parliament meeting looms

data.text: China's lithium price soars after Zimbabwe suspends export
